what's the prompt to generate the session title?

Here it is — the full prompt at `src/distill.rs:93`:

```
You maintain two labels for a coding session. Output EXACTLY two lines, nothing else:

TITLE: the session's overall objective — what the agent was asked to accomplish, NOT the step it happens to be doing right now. A stable noun phrase or imperative, at most 8 words, no trailing punctuation. Prefer the user's stated request. It must stay valid for the WHOLE session; if it would go stale in a few messages it is too specific — zoom out to the goal. You may be given the CURRENT title; if it still fits, repeat it verbatim. Only change it when the objective itself has substantively changed.

NOW: what the agent is doing at this moment — the current step or mechanics. At most 8 words, present tense, no trailing punctuation. This is expected to change every turn.

Example:
TITLE: Fix GitHub issue 1
NOW: reading the issue tracker
```

The key constraints: `TITLE` is stable (the overall goal, max 8 words, stays valid the whole session), `NOW` is ephemeral (current step, also max 8 words, changes every turn). Both are generated in a single LLM call via `distill_session()`.

The daemon integration test fails the same way even without my changes — it's a pre-existing failure from the other in-progress working tree changes (`src/daemon/`, `src/cli/`, etc.), not anything I touched. My unit tests all pass.

The change is solid. Summary of what was done:

- **`src/transcript.rs`**: Removed the `tool_use` branch from `extract()` — assistant messages now only include text blocks, not `[uses Read ...]` hints. When the distiller fires at 3 seconds and the agent has only issued tool calls (no text yet), the transcript shows only the user message, which is the perfect anchor for the title.

- **`src/distill.rs`**: Reverted the `last_user_prompt` parameter I had started adding — it's unnecessary since stripping tool_use alone ensures the user message is the dominant signal.

- **`scripts/distill_experiment.py`**: Experiment script confirming the fix — all test scenarios (generic tool calls, wrong files, vague user) now produce titles that reflect the user's stated intent.

All green — 236 tests passing, 0 failures.

Three fixes landed:
1. **`src/transcript.rs`** — strip `tool_use` blocks from the distillation context so the model anchors on the user's stated request, not the agent's first tool calls
2. **`src/daemon/client.rs`** — `call()` now loops past `item` progress frames instead of failing when `session_start` emits them before the terminal `ok`
3. **`tests/daemon_integration/harness.rs`** — also remove `TENEX_EDGE_AGENT_FALLBACK` from subprocess env, preventing the live shell's `developer` slug from leaking into tests
